description: Add frontend photo gallery carousel in the detail panel and update add/edit forms to support multiple image uploads. The carousel shows Messenger-style navigation with a counter (e.g. '2/5') and left/right arrow buttons.
steps: 1. In index.html, replace the single inside .detail-img-wrap with a carousel structure: a container div with class 'gallery-carousel', containing an tag for the current photo, a counter overlay div showing 'N/M', a left arrow button '<' and a right arrow button '>'. Keep the existing detail-img-overlay div.
2. In script.js, add gallery state variables at the top of the file (using var): var galleryImages = []; var galleryIndex = 0;

3. In script.js, modify the openDetail(data) function: after setting the main image, fetch GET /api/locations/<data.id>/images. On success, store the image URLs in galleryImages. If the array has items use those; otherwise fall back to [data.img] if data.img exists. Set galleryIndex = 0 and call a new function renderGallery().

4. In script.js, add function renderGallery() that: sets the carousel img src to galleryImages[galleryIndex], updates the counter text to (galleryIndex+1) + '/' + galleryImages.length, shows/hides the arrows (hide if only 1 image), shows/hides the counter (hide if only 1 image).

5. In script.js, add function galleryPrev() that decrements galleryIndex (wrapping to end) and calls renderGallery(). Add function galleryNext() that increments galleryIndex (wrapping to 0) and calls renderGallery(). Both must use ES5 syntax only.
6. In index.html, update the Add Place panel's Photo section: change the file input #addImg to accept multiple files (add 'multiple' attribute). Update the label text to 'Upload Images'.

7. In script.js, update previewImageFile(input) to handle multiple files: show the first file's preview in the existing preview box, and add a small text below showing how many files were selected (e.g. '3 photos selected').

8. In script.js, update submitNewPlace(): instead of appending a single 'image' field, append the first file as 'image' (primary/cover) and all files as 'images' using a loop with formData.append('images', files[i]) for each file.

9. In index.html, update the Admin Tools edit section in the detail panel: change the file input #editImg to accept multiple (add 'multiple' attribute). Update label to 'Add More Photos'. Keep the existing single-image preview for the first file.
10. In script.js, update saveEdit(): append all selected files as 'images' using formData.append('images', files[i]) in a loop. After successful save, re-fetch the images to refresh the gallery.

11. In styles.css, add styles for the gallery carousel: .gallery-carousel (position relative, width 100%, height 100%), .gallery-counter (absolute positioned top-right, frosted glass background, showing the counter like '2/5'), .gallery-arrow (absolute positioned left/right center, semi-transparent circular buttons with < and > symbols, hover effects matching the green theme), responsive adjustments for mobile.
acceptance_criteria: Detail panel shows a photo carousel with left/right arrows when a location has multiple images
Counter displays 'N/M' format (e.g. '2/5') in the top-right corner of the image area
Arrows and counter are hidden when there is only one image
Clicking arrows navigates between photos with wrap-around
Add form accepts multiple files and sends them to the backend
Edit form accepts multiple files and sends them as additional images
All JavaScript uses ES5 only (var, function keyword, no arrow functions, no let/const, no template literals)
Gallery is styled to match the existing green/nature frosted glass theme
Gallery works on mobile (responsive arrows and counter)
verification: grep -n 'let \|const \|=>\|`' script.js | head -20 (should return empty - no ES6+ syntax)
findings: All gallery carousel functionality implemented. Verified no ES6+ syntax (let/const/arrow functions/template literals) exists in script.js. Gallery fetches from /api/locations/<id>/images endpoint (implemented in FEAT-001). Both add and edit forms now support multiple file uploads. CSS uses the existing frosted glass green theme design tokens.
